tools.description: Suite of WiFi and Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32, including scanning, deauthentication, beacon spam, and probe capture.
Linux mac80211-compatible full-stack 802.11a/g/n SDR implementation on Xilinx FPGAs/Zynq with AD9361/ZC706 and ZCU102 reference designs.
C-based firmware patching framework for Broadcom/Cypress WiFi chips that adds monitor mode, frame injection, and CSI extraction.
Complete suite of tools to assess WiFi network security, including airodump-ng, aireplay-ng, airmon-ng, and aircrack-ng for WEP/WPA/WPA2-PSK.
Tool for capturing raw 802.11 frames including WPA/WPA2/WPA3 PMKIDs, SAE handshakes, and EAPOL for offline hashcat processing.
Small set of tools to convert captured WiFi frames (including WPA3 SAE) into hashcat/john-compatible hash formats.
Tool for performing targeted attacks on WPA2-Enterprise networks using the Extensible Authentication Protocol, including evil twin and credential harvesting.
Automated rogue access point framework that performs WiFi phishing (evil twin + captive portal) attacks to obtain credentials.
Modified hostapd with intelligent SSID cloaking, EAP-GTC/MD5/MSCHAPv2 relay, karma, and loud mode for rogue AP attacks.
Automated wireless auditor that captures WPA/WPA2 handshakes, PMKID, and WPS pixie-dust credentials against multiple targets.
Swiss Army knife for 802.11, BLE, IPv4 and IPv6 network reconnaissance and MITM attacks, including WiFi probing and client deauth.
Proof-of-concept scripts for the Dragonblood paper demonstrating timing/cache side-channel and downgrade attacks against WPA3 SAE.
Scripts to test whether 802.11 access points and clients are vulnerable to KRACK key-reinstallation attacks against the 4-way and group handshakes.
Proof-of-concept scripts for Fragmentation and Aggregation attacks (FragAttacks) against 802.11 implementations.
Experimental tool to test WPA3 SAE and EAP-pwd implementations for reflection, invalid-curve, and zero-scalar attacks.
Automated setup of a rogue access point for capturing WPA/WPA2 handshakes or performing KRACK key-reinstallation attacks.
WPA/WPA2 security auditing tool that retrieves keys by spawning a rogue AP with a captive portal and verifying the password against a captured handshake.
Automated wireless attack tool that performs WPS Pixie-Dust and online PIN brute-force attacks against vulnerable access points.
Out-of-tree ath10k and ath11k Linux drivers adding radiotap monitor mode and raw 802.11 frame injection for Wi-Fi 5 and Wi-Fi 6 chipsets.
Tool that tunnels EAP over RADIUS to perform evil twin attacks against WPA3-Enterprise networks by relaying authentication frames.
